content: Hi, I'm Kit and I'm a Professional Rock Climber and Guide. I'm currently learning Spanish in preparation for a move to Taiwan.
That's exciting! What inspired you to learn Spanish and move to Taiwan?

I've always been interested in different cultures and languages, and I have some friends who are from Taiwan. I've also been getting into origami and stand-up comedy, which has helped me meet new people and learn new skills.
Origami and stand-up comedy are both unique hobbies. How did you get into them and what do you enjoy about them?

I got into origami because I was looking for a new way to relax and challenge my mind. I love the precision and creativity of origami. As for stand-up comedy, I've always loved making people laugh and I thought it would be a great way to challenge myself and improve my public speaking skills.
That's great. Have you performed stand-up comedy in front of an audience before?
Yeah, I've done a few open mics and shows at local comedy clubs. It's a rush, but it's also a lot of fun. I'm planning to perform at a comedy club in Taipei when I move to Taiwan.
Taipei is a great city. What do you think you'll miss most about your current city when you move?

I'll miss my friends and family, of course, but I'll also miss the urban sketching community that I've been a part of. I love drawing and sketching, and it's been a great way to meet new people and learn about art.
Urban sketching sounds like a great hobby. Have you thought about joining any expat groups or clubs in Taiwan to meet new people and make friends?

Yeah, I've been looking into some expat groups and language exchange programs. I'm also planning to take some Mandarin classes when I get to Taiwan, which should be a great way to meet new people and learn a new language.
Mandarin is a challenging language, but it's also very rewarding. What's your plan for learning Mandarin when you move to Taiwan?
I've been using language learning apps and watching Taiwanese TV shows and movies to get a feel for the language. I'm also planning to take some language classes when I get to Taiwan.
That's a great plan. Immersion is one of the best ways to learn a language. Do you have any favorite Taiwanese foods or dishes that you're looking forward to trying?
Yeah, I love beef noodle soup and stinky tofu. I'm also looking forward to trying some of the street food and local markets in Taiwan.
Taiwanese food is delicious. What's your expected move date and how are you preparing for the transition?
I'll be moving in about 3 months, so I'm trying to get everything in order and make sure I have all the necessary documents and arrangements in place.
I'm excited for this new adventure and I'm looking forward to learning more about the culture and way of life in Taiwan.
It's great that you're excited about the move. I'm sure you'll have a great time in Taiwan and learn a lot about the culture and language.
